name: Pearl Street YMCA All Week Camp - Tropical Paradise
description: Week 1 of Pearl Street YMCA Summer Day Camp. Campers ages 5 to 12 spend Monday through Friday in age-based groups led by counselors and CITs. The week features tropical-themed games, swimming at least once, crafts, and small and large group activities. All-Week campers age 7 and up also pick a Specialty Camp (sports, climbing, arts, gymnastics, and similar) that meets 1 to 2:30 pm Monday through Thursday. Check-in 8:45 am, check-out 4 pm, with optional extended care 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm. Pack a nut-free lunch.

name: Pearl Street YMCA All Week Camp - Holiday Hoopla
description: Week 2 of Pearl Street YMCA Summer Day Camp. Holiday-themed games and group activities run Tuesday through Friday (closed July 4 weekend). Campers age 7 and up pick a Specialty Camp from options like Backyard Games, Creative Creations, Game Show, Lego Creations, Soccer, or Ynews. Day runs 8:45 am to 4 pm with optional 7 am to 6 pm extended care. Pack a nut-free lunch.

name: Pearl Street YMCA All Week Camp - Under the Big Top
description: Week 3 of Pearl Street YMCA Summer Day Camp, circus-themed week. Campers age 7 and up choose a Specialty Camp from options including Backyard Games, Dance Mix, Lego Creations, Mad Scientist, Pickleball, or Rockwall. Standard day is 8:45 am to 4 pm, extended care available 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm. Includes swimming once a week.

name: Pearl Street YMCA All Week Camp - Most Valuable Player
description: Week 5 sports-themed week at Pearl Street YMCA Summer Day Camp. Campers ages 5 to 12 join age-based groups for games, swimming once during the week, and crafts. Specialty Camps for ages 7 and up include Cheerleading, Backyard Games, Creative Creations, Dance Mix, Rockwall, or Soccer. Day runs 8:45 am to 4 pm with optional extended care 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm.

name: Pearl Street YMCA Itty Bitty Camp - Most Valuable Player
description: Half-day camp at Pearl Street YMCA designed for first-time campers ages 3 and 4. Mornings include games, group play, and simple themed activities tied to the Most Valuable Player sports week. Children must be toilet independent. Check-in 8:30 am, check-out 12 pm. No extended care option for Itty Bitty.

name: Pearl Street YMCA Mid Week Camp - Color Blast
description: Tuesday through Thursday partial-week option at Pearl Street YMCA, Week 10 (Color Blast theme). Campers ages 5 to 12 join classic camp games, group activities, and a colorful theme finale to the summer. Check-in 8:45 am, check-out 4 pm. Extended care available 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm.

name: Lakewood YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 1
description: Lakewood Family YMCA Summer Day Camp opening week. Camp runs Monday through Friday with age-based groups, weekly themes, swimming at least once a week, songs, crafts, and large and small group games. All Week campers age 7 and up choose a Specialty Camp meeting in the afternoon. Check-in 8:45 am, check-out 4 pm.

name: Lakewood YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 6
description: Week 6 of Lakewood Family YMCA Summer Day Camp. Campers ages 5 to 12 are placed in age-based groups led by counselors and CITs. Days include themed games, crafts, group play, and swimming once during the week. Itty Bitty (ages 3 to 4) and Mid Week options also offered. Standard day 8:45 am to 4 pm with optional 7 am to 6 pm extended care.

name: Mel Korum YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 1 Tropical Paradise
description: Mel Korum Family YMCA day camp in Puyallup, opening week. Campers join age-based groups for themed games, crafts, swimming once a week, and small and large group activities. Specialty Camp options for All Week campers age 7 and up. Day runs 8:45 am to 4 pm with extended care 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm. Itty Bitty (ages 3 to 4) and Mid Week options also offered.

name: Mel Korum YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 7 Around the World
description: Around-the-world themed week at Mel Korum Family YMCA Puyallup. Campers travel through cultures via games, crafts, and group activities, plus swimming once a week. Camp runs Monday through Friday in age-based groups led by counselors and CITs. Standard day 8:45 am to 4 pm with optional 7 am to 6 pm extended care. Specialty Camps available for ages 7 and up.

name: Gordon YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 4 Jurassic Jungle
description: Dinosaur-themed Week 4 at Gordon Family YMCA in Sumner. Campers ages 5 to 12 explore prehistoric games, crafts, and group activities, with swimming once during the week. Day runs 8:45 am to 4 pm with optional 7 am to 6 pm extended care. Specialty Camps available for ages 7 and up.

name: Gordon YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 8 Bug Bonanza
description: Nature and bug-themed Week 8 at Gordon Family YMCA Sumner. Outdoor games and group challenges, plus crafts, swimming, and small group play in age-based camp groups. Specialty Camps for ages 7 and up. Day 8:45 am to 4 pm with extended care 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm.

name: Tom Taylor YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 2 Holiday Hoopla
description: Tom Taylor Family YMCA in Gig Harbor, holiday-themed week. Campers ages 5 to 12 are grouped by age and led by counselors and CITs through games, crafts, and small and large group play, with swimming once during the week. Specialty Camps for ages 7 and up. Day 8:45 am to 4 pm with extended care 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm. Tuesday through Friday only (closed July 4 weekend).

name: Tom Taylor YMCA Summer Day Camp - Week 9 Wizards and Warlocks
description: Fantasy-themed Week 9 at Tom Taylor Family YMCA, Gig Harbor. Imagination-driven games, crafts, and group activities for ages 5 to 12 in age-based camp groups. Includes swimming once a week and a daily Specialty Camp for ages 7 and up. Day 8:45 am to 4 pm with optional extended care 7 to 8:30 am and 4:30 to 6 pm.

name: Federal Way K-8 Summer Camp - Camp-tastic Kickoff
description: Federal Way Community Center day camp for grades K through 8. Week 1 opens the summer with a field trip to Town Square Park in Federal Way. Daily activities include swimming and rock climbing at the community center, group games, and outdoor play. Camp follows the Federal Way Public Schools calendar. First-come, first-served registration.

name: Federal Way K-8 Summer Camp - Pirate Palooza
description: Pirate-themed week of K-8 Summer Camp at Federal Way Community Center. Week 3 includes a field trip to Les Gove Park in Auburn. Campers swim, climb, and join group games and crafts at the community center. First-come, first-served registration through Federal Way Parks & Recreation.

name: Federal Way K-8 Summer Camp - Game On!
description: Sports and games-themed Week 5 of Federal Way Community Center K-8 Summer Camp. Field trip to West Fenwick Park in Kent during the week. Campers also swim and rock climb at the community center, with group games, crafts, and outdoor play. First-come, first-served.

name: Federal Way K-8 Summer Camp - Camp Hollywood
description: Movie and entertainment-themed Week 9 of K-8 Summer Camp at Federal Way Community Center. Field trip to Paradise Lanes in Tacoma. Campers swim and rock climb at the community center, plus group games, crafts, and outdoor activities. First-come, first-served registration.

name: Federal Way Fireflies Preschool Camp - Pirate Adventure
description: Preschool day camp at Federal Way Community Center for ages 3 to 5. Pirate-themed Week 3 includes simple themed crafts, group play, story time, and active games on a consistent Monday through Friday schedule. Designed to support friendship and independence in a young camp setting.

name: Federal Way Fireflies Preschool Camp - Lights, Camera, Camp
description: Federal Way Community Center preschool camp for ages 3 to 5. Final week of Fireflies camp features movie-themed crafts, group play, and active games Monday through Friday in a consistent preschool environment.

name: Federal Way DCIT Leaders-in-Training - Leadership Launchpad
description: Day Camp Leaders-in-Training program for teens ages 13 to 15 at Federal Way Community Center. Week 1 focuses on leadership skills with a field trip to Les Gove Park in Auburn. DCITs spend the week training and supporting day camp leaders in the K-8 Summer Camp, building leadership and problem-solving skills.

name: Federal Way DCIT Leaders-in-Training - Activity Masters
description: Final week of Federal Way DCIT (Day Camp Leaders-in-Training) program for teens ages 13 to 15. Field trip to Titlow Park in Tacoma. Teens lead activities and apply leadership skills built earlier in the summer with K-8 day campers.

name: Federal Way Mini Dance Camp
description: Specialty dance camp at Federal Way Community Center for kids who love musicals. Campers sing, dance, and step into the world of their favorite stage shows. Tuesday through Friday session. Specialty Summer Camps are managed separately from the regular K-8 day camp; contact Cassie Swan for questions.

name: Federal Way Spongebob Theatre Camp
description: Two-week musical theatre camp at Federal Way Community Center based on Spongebob Squarepants. Campers learn songs, choreography, and acting as Bikini Bottom faces an erupting volcano. Specialty Summer Camp run separately from the regular day camp program. Contact Cassie Swan for camp questions.

name: Federal Way Ballerina Dream Camp
description: Beginner ballet camp at Federal Way Community Center for young dancers. Campers explore ballet through imagination, creative movement, and beginner-friendly technique over a four-day session. Run by the Specialty Summer Camp program.

name: Boys & Girls Club Lakewood Summer Program - Week 1
description: Boys & Girls Club Lakewood Branch summer program at the Gary & Carol Milgard Family Hope Center. Monday through Friday 8 am to 5:30 pm with games, creative projects, sports, homework help, nutritious meals and snacks, technology access, STEM activities, and field trips. Reduced and free lunch pricing available; financial aid offered. No child turned away for inability to pay.

name: Boys & Girls Club Lakewood Summer Program - Week 5
description: Mid-summer week of Boys & Girls Club Lakewood Branch summer program. Daily 8 am to 5:30 pm with arts, games, sports, STEM exploration, field trips, and homework help. Reduced and free lunch options available. Financial aid available; no child turned away for inability to pay.

name: Boys & Girls Club Al Davies Summer Program - Week 3
description: Al Davies Branch summer program in Tacoma. Monday through Friday 8 am to 5:30 pm with arts and music, sports, STEM activities, technology access, homework help, and meals and snacks. Reduced and free lunch available. Financial aid offered; no child turned away for inability to pay.

name: Boys & Girls Club Al Davies Summer Program - Week 8
description: Late-summer week at Al Davies Branch in Tacoma. Daily 8 am to 5:30 pm program with sports, arts, STEM, music, field trips, and homework support. Reduced and free lunch available. Financial aid offered.

name: Boys & Girls Club D.A. Gonyea Summer Program - Week 2
description: Boys & Girls Club D.A. Gonyea Branch summer program in north Tacoma. Monday through Friday 8 am to 5:30 pm with sports, arts and music, STEM activities, field trips, technology access, and homework help. Meals and snacks included. Financial aid available; no child turned away for inability to pay.

name: Boys & Girls Club Henry T. Schatz Summer Program - Week 4
description: Henry T. Schatz Branch summer program in south Tacoma. Daily 8 am to 5:30 pm Monday through Friday with sports, arts, STEM, music, technology access, homework help, and meals and snacks. Reduced and free lunch available. Financial aid offered.

name: Skyhawks Soccer Full-Day Camp at Vassault Park
description: Skyhawks full-day soccer camp at Vassault Park run through Parks Tacoma. Five-day Monday through Friday session 9 am to 3 pm covering ball control, passing, shooting, defense, and game play. Open to ages 6 to 12. Registration via Parks Tacoma ActiveNet.

name: Skyhawks Mini-Hawk Camp at Norpoint Park
description: Skyhawks Mini-Hawk introductory multi-sport camp through Parks Tacoma at Norpoint Park. Five-day half-day session 9 am to 12 pm introducing baseball, basketball, and soccer to ages 4 to 7 in a noncompetitive setting focused on motor skills, listening, and teamwork.

name: Skyhawks Tennis Camp at Jefferson Park
description: Half-day Skyhawks tennis camp at Jefferson Park run by Parks Tacoma. Monday through Friday 9 am to 12 pm session for ages 7 to 12 covering forehand, backhand, serving, scoring, and match play. Registration through Parks Tacoma ActiveNet.

name: DB Skimboarding Summer Camp - Week 1 Afternoon
description: DB Skimboarding Camp at Point Defiance Marina, run with Parks Tacoma. Five-day Monday through Friday 1 pm to 3:30 pm session in a shallow water pool. Open to ages 6 to 16, beginners and progressing riders. Campers learn to ride, balance, and slide using flatland skimboards. Note: this camp is not eligible for financial aid.

name: DB Skimboarding Summer Camp - Week 3 Morning
description: July session of DB Skimboarding Camp at Point Defiance Marina, run with Parks Tacoma. Monday through Friday 10 am to 12 pm in a shallow water pool. Open to ages 6 to 16. Campers learn to ride, balance, and slide using flatland skimboards in a noncompetitive setting. Not eligible for financial aid.

name: Baseball Camp Full Day at Heidelberg Sports Complex
description: Full-day baseball camp at Heidelberg Sports Complex through Parks Tacoma. Monday through Friday 9 am to 4 pm for ages 6 to 12 with drills, fielding, hitting, base running, and game play.

name: Skyhawks Basketball Half-Day Camp at Kandle Park
description: Skyhawks half-day Mini-Hawk style basketball camp at Kandle Park run by Parks Tacoma. Tuesday through Wednesday short-week session (3 days) 9 am to 12 pm for ages 4 to 7 introducing dribbling, passing, shooting, and team play in a noncompetitive setting.

name: Skyhawks Soccer Full-Day Camp at Norpoint Park
description: Three-day Skyhawks full-day soccer camp at Norpoint Park run by Parks Tacoma. Monday through Wednesday 9 am to 3 pm for ages 6 to 12 covering passing, shooting, dribbling, defense, and small-sided games. Registration via Parks Tacoma ActiveNet.

name: Soccer Camp Full Day at SERA Sports Complex
description: Full-day soccer camp at SERA Sports Complex run through Parks Tacoma. Monday through Friday 9 am to 4 pm for ages 6 to 12 covering technical skills, small-sided games, and full-field scrimmages.

name: Little All Stars Camp at Norpoint Park - Week 1
description: Half-day introductory multi-sport camp at Norpoint Park through Parks Tacoma. Monday through Friday 9 am to 12 pm for ages 4 to 6. Campers try basic sports skills, group games, and movement activities in a beginner-friendly setting.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Tiny Turtles Camp - Late June
description: Two-day preschool zoo camp at Point Defiance Zoo & Aquarium for ages 4 and 5. Campers learn about wildlife through stories, play, and animal encounters. Thursday and Friday session 9:15 am to 3 pm.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Tiny Turtles Camp - Mid July
description: Two-day preschool zoo camp at Point Defiance Zoo & Aquarium for ages 4 and 5. Stories, play, and discovery alongside meet-and-greets with animals from around the world. Thursday and Friday 9:15 am to 3 pm.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Little Lemurs Camp - Late June
description: Three-day Zoo camp at Point Defiance for kindergarten graduates. Campers explore animals around the Zoo using sight, hearing, and touch, learning what each species needs to thrive. Monday through Wednesday 9:15 am to 3 pm.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Animal Investigators Camp - July
description: Five-day Zoo camp at Point Defiance for 1st and 2nd grade graduates. Campers examine skulls, study beaks, compare adaptations of marine and land animals, and have close encounters with Zoo animals to see how care teams support them. Monday through Friday 9 am to 3:15 pm.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Budding Biologists Camp - August
description: Five-day Zoo camp at Point Defiance for 1st and 2nd grade graduates. Campers learn how biologists study living things and try the tools scientists use in the lab and field, while seeing how Zoo staff care for the animals. Monday through Friday 9 am to 3:15 pm.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Wild Explorers Camp - July
description: Five-day Zoo camp at Point Defiance for 3rd and 4th grade graduates. Campers tour the Zoo, Aquariums, and surrounding natural areas to study how animals adapt to terrestrial and marine environments and meet animals up close. Monday through Friday 8:45 am to 3:30 pm.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Science Explorers Camp - July
description: Four-day Zoo camp at Point Defiance for 5th to 7th grade graduates. Campers explore animal adaptations, tour Zoo and aquarium habitats, and learn how care teams and veterinarians work, with some classroom learning. Tuesday through Friday 8:45 am to 3:45 pm.

name: Point Defiance Zoo Eco Explorers Camp - July
description: Four-day Zoo camp at Point Defiance for 8th to 11th grade graduates. Teen campers study animal adaptations, tour Zoo and aquarium habitats, and learn how Zoo staff care for animals. Some classroom learning included. Tuesday through Friday 8:45 am to 3:45 pm.

name: Greentrike Summer Camp Full Day - July 6 Week
description: Greentrike summer camp at the Children's Museum of Tacoma. Monday through Thursday full-day session for ages 3 to 7. Each week features a fusion of art, science, engineering, storytelling, and adventure facilitated by two Greentrike educators. Children must be toilet independent. No nap provided. 9 am to 3 pm.

name: Greentrike Summer Camp Half Day - July 6 Week
description: Greentrike half-day summer camp at the Children's Museum of Tacoma. Monday through Thursday 9 am to 12 pm for ages 3 to 7. Camp blends art, science, engineering, storytelling, and play, run by two Greentrike educators in a small group. Children must be toilet independent.

name: Greentrike Summer Camp Full Day - July 20 Week
description: Greentrike summer camp at the Children's Museum of Tacoma, full-day option. Monday through Thursday 9 am to 3 pm for ages 3 to 7. Each themed week mixes art, science, engineering, storytelling, and outdoor play, led by two educators in a small camp group. Children must be toilet independent.

name: Greentrike Summer Camp Full Day - August 3 Week
description: Greentrike summer camp at the Children's Museum of Tacoma. Full-day Monday through Thursday session for ages 3 to 7. Themed art, science, engineering, storytelling, and play activities led by two Greentrike educators. Children must be toilet independent.

name: Greentrike Summer Camp Half Day - August 17 Week
description: Greentrike half-day summer camp at the Children's Museum of Tacoma. Monday through Thursday 9 am to 12 pm for ages 3 to 7. A weekly theme blends art, science, engineering, and storytelling, facilitated by two educators. Children must be toilet independent.

name: Greentrike Summer Camp Full Day - August 24 Week
description: Final week of Greentrike summer camp at the Children's Museum of Tacoma. Full-day Monday through Thursday for ages 3 to 7. Mixed art, science, engineering, storytelling, and play activities led by two Greentrike educators. Children must be toilet independent.
